docs.title: class Activity
docs.text: Garmin activity.

docs.title: method __init__
docs.text:
__init__(
    activity_type: Annotated[str, ActivityField(garmin_field='activityType/typeKey', aggregate=<AggFunc.FIRST: 'first'>)],
    location_name: Annotated[str, ActivityField(garmin_field='', aggregate=<AggFunc.FIRST: 'first'>)],
    duration: Annotated[Optional[float], ActivityField(garmin_field='', aggregate=<AggFunc.SUM: <built-in function sum>>)],
    average_hr: Annotated[Optional[float], ActivityField(garmin_field='averageHR', aggregate=<AggFunc.AVERAGE: 'average'>)] = None,
    calories: Annotated[Optional[float], ActivityField(garmin_field='', aggregate=<AggFunc.SUM: <built-in function sum>>)] = None,
    distance: Annotated[Optional[float, int], ActivityField(garmin_field='', aggregate=<AggFunc.SUM: <built-in function sum>>)] = None,
    elevation_gain: Annotated[Optional[float], ActivityField(garmin_field='', aggregate=<AggFunc.SUM: <built-in function sum>>)] = None,
    max_hr: Annotated[Optional[float], ActivityField(garmin_field='maxHR', aggregate=<AggFunc.MAX: <built-in function max>>)] = None,
    max_speed: Annotated[Optional[float], ActivityField(garmin_field='', aggregate=<AggFunc.MAX: <built-in function max>>)] = None,
    average_speed: Annotated[Optional[float], ActivityField(garmin_field='', aggregate=<AggFunc.MAX: <built-in function max>>)] = None,
    start_time: Annotated[Optional[str], ActivityField(garmin_field='startTimeLocal', aggregate=<AggFunc.MIN: <built-in function min>>)] = None,
    steps: Annotated[Optional[int], ActivityField(garmin_field='', aggregate=<AggFunc.SUM: <built-in function sum>>)] = None,
    moving_duration: Annotated[Optional[float], ActivityField(garmin_field='', aggregate=<AggFunc.SUM: <built-in function sum>>)] = None,
    non_walking_steps: Annotated[Optional[int], ActivityField(garmin_field=None, aggregate=<AggFunc.SUM: <built-in function sum>>)] = None,
    sport: Annotated[Optional[str], ActivityField(garmin_field=None, aggregate=<AggFunc.FIRST: 'first'>)] = None,
    comment: Annotated[Optional[str], ActivityField(garmin_field=None, aggregate=<AggFunc.FIRST: 'first'>)] = None
) → None

docs.title: method estimate_steps
docs.text:
estimate_steps() → int

Estimate steps for the activity.

Garmin do not provide steps for some activities. Actually it erroneously calculate steps during such activities like roller skiing. We have to estimate this steps to subtract them from Walking activity steps.

docs.title: method detect_sport
docs.text:
detect_sport(activity: Activity) → Tuple[str, bool]

Detect sport.

Return (sport, separate)

If the activity looks like significant one we want to see it separately. For example we would like to aggregate all small bicycle trips but not the big training one.

docs.title: function search_missed_steps_in_sheet
docs.text:
search_missed_steps_in_sheet(
    fitness: Worksheet,
    rows: List[List[str]],
    columns: ColumnsMapper
) → None

Add missed in Garmin API steps data from earlier entered in the spreadsheet.

We need that only if we need very old data - for some reason Garmin API do not return steps more than for last three months. May be we can use some flag to get them - in the Garmin App you can see this steps but not from the API. Anyway in such situation if we already had entered steps manually in the Google Sheet we can find and use them.

Just once I needed this mode so I keep it in the code. If you have steps from Garmin API we won't read the Google Sheet to Pandas DataFrame so this code won't take any resources.

docs.title: module snake_to_camel
docs.text: Convert names from Python (snake-name) to Java (camelName) name convention and vice-versa.

docs.title: function snake_to_camel

snake_to_camel(snake_case_name: str) → str

Convert snake_case_names to camelCase.

docs.title: function camel_to_upper_snake
docs.text:
camel_to_upper_snake(name: str) → str

Convert camelCase to UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.

camel_to_upper_snake('camel2_camel2_case') 'CAMEL2_CAMEL2_CASE'

camel_to_upper_snake('getHTTPResponseCode') 'GET_HTTP_RESPONSE_CODE'

camel_to_upper_snake('HTTPResponseCodeXYZ') 'HTTP_RESPONSE_CODE_XYZ'
